commit.message:
feat: add .NET MAUI Windows app with full business management UI (#31)

Complete MAUI application covering all internal API functionality:

- Auth: Login, Register with tenant selection
- Dashboard: Stats overview + recent activities
- CRM: Companies, Contacts, Deals, Activities (CRUD + search + pagination)
- HR: Employees (CRUD + department filter + deactivation)
- Catalog: Products (with variants/images/ingredients), Categories
- Invoicing: Invoices (create/send/cancel/void/payments), Settings
- Time Tracking: Time entries (submit/approve/reject), Entry types
- Admin: User management, Tenant settings

Architecture:
- 33 ViewModels with CommunityToolkit.Mvvm source generators
- 33 XAML pages with dark theme (Warp Business brand)
- ApiClient service (490+ lines covering all endpoints)
- JWT auth with SecureStorage + auto-refresh via DelegatingHandler
- Shell flyout navigation with registered detail/edit routes
- Full DI setup in MauiProgram.cs
- Targets net10.0-windows (also supports Android, iOS, macOS)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent browser caching of raw file endpoint on repeated saves

Root cause of 'second save does not work':
- The JS fetches /api/v1/workspace/raw/<file_id> AFTER each save to get
  the exported bytes, then writes them to the local file via fsHandle.
- The file_id never changes within a session, so the browser cached the
  first save's response and returned stale bytes on every subsequent save.
- Result: the local file was re-written with the FIRST save's bytes each
  time, making it appear unchanged.

Fix (two-layer):
1. Server: raw_file() now returns Cache-Control: no-store headers
2. JS: raw fetch URL includes ?_=<timestamp> cache-buster

Verified with Playwright: save1 writes 36597 bytes, save2 writes 36614
bytes (different content), both workspace file and local file are updated.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Koto
repository.description: AI Agent

commit.message:
fix: use showSaveFilePicker on first Ctrl+S to save to original location

Previously, Ctrl+S on a workspace-imported file only wrote to workspace/
with no way to update the user's original file — because drag-drop and
file-input uploads don't provide a FileSystemFileHandle.

New behaviour:
- First Ctrl+S with no existing handle → showSaveFilePicker() dialog
  (called BEFORE any other await so browser user-gesture activation is live)
  - User picks a save location; handle stored in tab.fsHandle + _fsHandleMap
  - User cancels → save aborted cleanly
- Every subsequent Ctrl+S → reuses stored handle, no dialog
- Files opened via the Open-Local-File picker already have a handle so
  they skip the dialog entirely (unchanged behaviour)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Koto
repository.description: AI Agent

commit.message:
fix: store fsHandle in _fsHandleMap to survive tab object replacement

When a file is opened via _openFilePicker, the FileSystemFileHandle was only
stored in the tab object (state.openTabs[i].fsHandle). If that tab entry
was ever replaced (e.g., Router.load called again for same path without
_fsHandle), the handle would be lost and subsequent Ctrl+S saves would not
write back to the original file on disk.

Fix: maintain a separate _fsHandleMap (Map<wsPath, FileSystemFileHandle>).
- Populate it in Router.load whenever file._fsHandle is set
- In saveFile, use (tab.fsHandle || _fsHandleMap.get(wsPath)) as fallback
- All state is captured before any awaits to avoid async race conditions

Tab title now shows [SAVE] N chars | fsHandle=true/false for debugging.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Koto
repository.description: AI Agent

commit.message:
fix: capture tab+fsHandle before first await in saveFile

Root cause: saveFile() called state.openTabs.find() and used tab.fsHandle
AFTER an await — meaning any async operation that ran in the gap
(e.g. the autoSave timer that fires 2s after each edit) could theoretically
clear or replace the tab reference before we checked fsHandle, causing
the raw bytes fetch and FileSystemFileHandle write-back to never run.

Fix: capture _saveTab, _saveFsHandle, _saveFileId, _saveFileType, _saveWsPath
before the very first await. These local variables are immune to any
state mutations that happen during the async fetch.

Debug: document.title now shows '[SAVE] N chars | fsHandle=true/false' so
we can verify both the content length and the handle state on each save.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace btn.disabled guard with _isSaving flag in saveFile

Root cause: saveFile() used btn.disabled as its re-entry guard:
  if (btn.disabled) return;
But other code paths (e.g. initial page load, _closeTab, deleteWorkspaceFile)
also set wa-save-btn.disabled=true. After the first explicit save completed,
something was leaving the button in a disabled state, causing every subsequent
Ctrl+S to silently return at the guard check.

Server logs confirmed: only ONE explicit=True save ever reached the server,
even though autoSave (explicit=False) continued to work fine — proving the
editor and serialize() were functional, but saveFile() was blocked at entry.

Fix: use a private JS variable _isSaving instead of the DOM button state.
This is immune to interference from other code paths that toggle btn.disabled
for UI reasons.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: update Bart history with sound plan v1.1 consolidation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: sound plan v1.1 — 10 blockers + 11 concerns resolved from 7-agent review

Consolidated findings from all 7 reviewers (Bart, CBG, Marge, Chalmers,
Flanders, Smithers, Moe) into a single v1.1 pass of the implementation plan.

Blockers resolved (10):
- Marge #2: Nelson LLM test scenarios now explicitly defined (5 scenarios)
- Marge #3: Regression baseline snapshot protocol added to all gates
- Marge #4: Headless mode coverage added to all gate criteria
- Chalmers #3: CBG WAVE-1 parallelism clarified (non-blocking to gate)
- Chalmers #4: GATE-0 interface contract freeze specified
- Chalmers #5: WAVE-3 rollback plan documented
- Flanders #3: GUID pre-assignment protocol added before WAVE-1
- Flanders #4 + Moe #3: Field naming convention standardized
- Flanders #5: Creature death state lifecycle clarified
- Smithers #3: Verb integration pattern specified (effects pipeline primary)

Concerns resolved (11):
- Bart C1: Terminal driver switched from os.execute() to io.popen()
- Bart C2: Dual integration path resolved (effects pipeline canonical)
- Bart C3: Crossfade fade_in_ms/fade_out_ms added to driver contract
- Bart C4: Board/plan parallelism reconciled (WAVE-2 Track 2A after GATE-0)
- Bart C5: .ogg standardized to .opus everywhere
- Bart C6: Sound key resolution chain documented
- Bart C7: scan_object lifecycle timing specified

fix: use MutationObserver to track editor content changes reliably

WangEditor's onChange + getHtml() may not capture user edits reliably.
Replace with a MutationObserver on the contenteditable DOM element, which
fires synchronously on every character typed, paste, or format change.

- render(): attach MutationObserver to contenteditable div, update _lastHtml on every mutation
- destroy(): disconnect observer to prevent memory leaks
- serialize(): compare getHtml() vs MutationObserver _lastHtml; pick the longer one
- Debug: serialize() logs all three lengths so we can diagnose in DevTools
- Server: log HTML char count and explicit/auto flag on every auto_save call
- Toast: show '已保存 (N chars)' to confirm content length without DevTools

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: capture-phase Ctrl+S prevents WangEditor from swallowing the event

WangEditor, when focused, calls stopPropagation() on keydown events
before they bubble up to document. This means our document-level Ctrl+S
handler was never called while the editor was focused (i.e., the second
save always failed because user had clicked into the editor to type).

Fix: register the keydown listener with capture:true so it runs in the
capture phase (top-down, before WangEditor's bubble-phase handler).
Also add stopPropagation() to prevent WangEditor from processing Ctrl+S.
Also add early return if btn.disabled to prevent concurrent saves.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: delete file closes its tab to prevent auto-save recreating it

Root cause: deleting a workspace file while it was open in a tab would
let the auto-save timer fire and write the file back to disk via
auto_save (which has ws_source_path). Also open_file was always
re-copying the file to workspace/ even when opened from the panel.

Fixes:
- deleteWorkspaceFile: close & remove the tab, cancel pending auto-save
  timer, switch to neighbor tab or reset canvas if no tabs left
- open_file: skip workspace copy when request includes ws_path field
  (set by JS when opening a file already in the workspace)
- JS Router.load: pass ws_path in FormData when file._wsPath is set

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: assisted-ai-creator <271429067+assisted-ai-creator@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: save directly to original file using File System Access API

When a user opens a file via the file picker, the browser's
FileSystemFileHandle is stored on the tab. On Ctrl+S / Save:
1. Editor content is serialized and sent to auto_save (server round-trip
   converts HTML back to proper .docx/.xlsx bytes)
2. Bytes are fetched from /api/v1/workspace/raw/<file_id>
3. Written directly to the original file on disk via fileHandle.createWritable()

Falls back gracefully if: API not supported (old browser), user
denies write permission, or file was opened from the workspace panel.

Also wires dropZone click, leftDrop click, and 打开文件 button to use
showOpenFilePicker() so the handle is captured from the start.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add ML-powered admin insights engine

Add lib/ml/admin-insights.ts with 7 analytics functions:
- getDemandForecast: weighted moving avg + day-of-week seasonality
- getRevenueTrendAnalysis: moving averages, Z-score anomaly detection
- getItemPerformanceScores: multi-factor 0-100 scoring system
- getWasteAnalysis: cancellation patterns and revenue impact
- getCustomerSegmentation: RFM-inspired customer bucketing

commit.message:
fix: xlsx tab restore extracts sheets array; docx cache validates content; bust js cache

- _switchToTab: xlsx render uses data.sheets (not raw {sheets,_images} object)
  which was causing Luckysheet to initialize wrong and serialize() to return strings
- _switchToTab: docx render skips empty/br-only cache, falls back to serverData.html
- Add random query param to JS script tag to bust browser cache on each page load

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: keep _lastHtml in sync on onChange to prevent stale content saves

- Update _lastHtml on every WangEditor onChange event so serialize()
  fallback always returns the latest user content (not render-time content)
- Fix _switchToTab: skip background disk save if cache is empty string
  to prevent overwriting workspace file with empty docx

Root cause: when getHtml() momentarily returned empty during auto-save,
_lastHtml (set at render time) was used as fallback, reverting edits.
The log confirmed 36629-byte saves being overwritten by 36600-byte saves.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit: Fini expense tracker Android app

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Migrate to TypeScript + SSR with next-redux-wrapper

- Add tsconfig.json (strict: false, allowJs: true for gradual migration)
- Add types/index.ts with shared interfaces
- Install next-redux-wrapper for SSR+Redux integration
- Convert store/store.js → store/store.ts with makeStore factory
- Add store/hooks.ts with typed useAppDispatch/useAppSelector
- Wrap _app.tsx with wrapper.withRedux
- Add getServerSideProps to 5 public pages (index, l/[id], lc/[name], a/[id], search/[key])
- Rename all pages/*.js(x) → .tsx (16 pages)
- Rename all store/feature/*.js → .ts (12 slices)
- Add NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL to .env.local

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: surface save errors + ensure parent dir exists before writing

- auto_save: add explicit=true flag for user-triggered saves — returns
  500 with error message instead of silent ok:true on write failure
- Promote debug logs to INFO so they appear in production logs
- mkdir parents before writing source file (handles missing subdirs)
- JS saveFile: pass explicit:true, check src_written in response,
  show warning toast if workspace file could not be written

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
